title: Weihea microphylla

The plant is native to Australia and is often found in dry and open habitats. Weihea microphylla is a low-growing shrub with small leaves and produces blue or purple flowers. Further classification of the plant is at the infraspecific level, where it can be divided into subspecies based on geographical variations.

intro: Weihea microphylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties.
